description:
After ir_typer::type_module() populates ModuleTypes.specs, validate each declared @spec against the inferred specs for that fn. Option 1 semantics: declared is an **upper bound** on inferred.

## Semantics

For a fn `foo` with declared @spec `params: Vec<Descr>, result: Descr`:

For each registered SpecId (sid, ft) in SpecRegistry where ft is for foo:
  inferred_arrow = (ft's param Descrs, ft's return Descr)
  declared_arrow = (declared.params, declared.result)
  
  // option 1 check: every inferred input is a subset of the declared input,
  //                 and the inferred output is a subset of the declared output
  if NOT (inferred.params[i] ⊆ declared.params[i] for all i)
     OR NOT (inferred.result ⊆ declared.result):
       emit diagnostic (use .20.5 taxonomy, .20.8 Descr Display)

Narrower inferred is OK (success-typing). Disjoint or wider is an error.

## Depends on fz-ul4.29.10

Critical: without on-demand any-key registration, every fn has an any-key spec with `Descr::any()` inputs. A user-declared narrow `@spec foo(integer) :: integer` would then *always* fail the check against the any-key (Descr::any() ⊄ integer). The on-demand change ensures the any-key only exists for fns that genuinely need it (closure/spawn/receive targets), and in that case the conflict with a narrow @spec is a real error worth surfacing.

## Where it lives

src/ir_codegen.rs (or a new src/spec_check.rs called from there): runs after type_module() returns, before per-spec compile_fn loop at line 1432.

## Tests

- Declared @spec exactly matches inferred: OK
- Declared @spec is wider than inferred (success-typing): OK, no warning by default
- Declared @spec is narrower than inferred (user lied about inputs they accept): error
- Declared @spec input is disjoint from inferred (e.g. declared :: float, inferred :: integer): error
- Declared @spec result is narrower than inferred: error
- A fn with @spec foo(integer) :: integer that's also a closure target: error (any-key with Descr::any() conflicts with declared integer input) — confirms .29.10 interaction
- A purely-direct fn with narrow @spec and matching narrow inferred: OK, no any-key in the picture

## Diagnostic shape

Use .20.8's Descr Display:

```
error: @spec violation for fn add1/1
   spec: integer -> integer
   inferred: float -> float  (from callsite at foo.fz:42)
   note: declared spec must be a superset of inferred behavior
```

comments.text:
Update from .29.10 investigation: any-keys are typer-internal fallback constructs, not user-facing claims. Validation must SKIP any-key entries when comparing declared @spec against registered specs — only narrow specs participate.

Rationale: a user-written `@spec foo(integer) :: integer` is a claim about typed input domains. The any-key spec (key = [Descr::any(); arity]) represents "what if all args are unknown" — a degenerate state useful inside the typer / for closure / spawn / receive landing pads, but not something the user is declaring against. Comparing declared against any-key would produce a category-error rejection.

Implementation: when iterating registered specs for a fn in the validation pass, filter out the entry where key == vec![Descr::any(); arity]. Compare against narrow specs only.

This replaces the previous dependency on .29.10 (which is now blocked on the larger typed-dispatch effort .29.12).

title: Validation pass: inferred ⊆ declared
